free_text: Yields of products from fast neutron-induced fission
of U-233 measured by means of an Isotope Separator
analysis method for fiss. yield determination
Neutron flux filtered by B4-C layer of 2 cm thickness
to cut off thermal and epithermal neutrons.
Average neutron energy in spectrum calculated by DORT
code is 1.19 MeV, and 1.25 MeV calculated by MCNP
codes respectively with maximum flux 1.7E11 n/cm2/sec.
Energy is taken as average value 1.22 MeV
B4-C filter gave good agreement between measured and
Multi spectrum scaling technique of four time
groups. Ion beam was collected on moveable Al-coated
plastic tape in front of spectrometry system.
Two Ge spectrometers of 50% and 80% relative
efficiency, shield from room background with bricks of
Fe,Pb and borated paraffin, from beta-particles - by
Plastic scintillator for monitoring of total
Porous graphite cylinder impregnated with U-233.

Obtained relative values of fission yields were
normalized by comparing yield value with known yield
Analysis is based on the code of G.Rudstam,
ratio of ETA-parent to ETA-daughter = MISC2 in 1/sec.
ETA=number of atoms collected/number of atoms formed.
